Event: Dad prioritizes daughter’s safety over Billy’s crusade
Explanation: Protecting his child and pulling back from a dangerous crusade benefits him and those he’s responsible for.

Event: Marvin clearly rejects Butcher’s surprise visit after years apart
Explanation: By shutting Butcher down, he protects himself and indirectly limits Butcher’s trouble for others.

Event: M.M. snaps “Oh hell no, the fuck do you want?” at Butcher
Explanation: His hostility reinforces a boundary that keeps him safer from Butcher’s schemes with minimal harm to others.

Event: M.M. and Annie bond over missing parents
Explanation: Emotional bonding strengthens trust and support for both of them.

Event: Soldier Boy throws a car that kills Marvin’s grandfather and family
Explanation: He stops car thieves (serving his image/mission) while lethally harming innocent bystanders.

Event: M.M. tells Butcher he’s “just a motherfucker”
Explanation: Honest characterization helps define boundaries and expectations in the partnership.

Event: M.M. later tells Butcher he’s “a motherfucker with a heart”
Explanation: Recognition of Butcher’s growth improves their working relationship and mutual trust.

Event: Billy’s visit about Soldier Boy compels Marvin to rejoin the group
Explanation: Billy gets his competent ally back while Marvin sacrifices safety and family stability to reenter danger.

Event: Marvin decides to rejoin The Boys to pursue closure on Soldier Boy
Explanation: His need for closure pulls him back into lethal risks with only uncertain benefit for anyone.

Event: M.M. constantly corrals Frenchie to follow plans
Explanation: His managing reduces chaos and increases mission safety at personal stress cost.

Event: M.M. thanks Frenchie deadpan for disgusting trivia
Explanation: Defusing Frenchie’s weirdness with humor maintains team cohesion at little cost.

Event: M.M. gives Kimiko pointers on how to cleanly break Hughie’s arm
Explanation: He helps orchestrate controlled harm to Hughie to serve operational needs more than Hughie’s welfare.

Event: M.M. wears sunglasses to go incognito
Explanation: Disguise improves his operational security without hurting others.

Event: M.M. insults Frenchie and another teammate as “French whore and Steven fucking Hawking”
Explanation: Snark vents his frustration and asserts status while mildly undermining team morale.

Event: M.M. calls out Butcher’s fake niceness and demands he “cut the small talk”
Explanation: Forcing honesty lets him evaluate the real proposal and keeps the team’s dealings clearer.

Event: Webweaver offers to pay M.M. “in nature” for the heroin enema
Explanation: The offer disgusts M.M. and doesn’t materially help anyone beyond fleeting gratification.

Event: M.M. welcomes Hughie into the group
Explanation: He accurately sees Hughie’s potential, benefiting Hughie and strengthening the team.

Event: M.M. befriends Annie and calls her first when Soldier Boy appears
Explanation: Trusting and including Annie improves coordination and safety with no shown downside.

Event: M.M. makes a risky power play to convince A-Train to flip
Explanation: He risks himself to pressure A-Train, and the gamble pays off when A-Train later saves Hughie.

Event: M.M. spares Countess Crow and lets her leave
Explanation: He correctly judges she’s less culpable and avoids needless killing, at little operational cost.

Event: M.M. refuses to cross certain moral lines
Explanation: Holding boundaries costs him some tactical advantages but prevents greater moral and collateral damage.

Event: Butcher recruits Soldier Boy against Homelander
Explanation: He gains a powerful ally at the price of unleashing another dangerous Supe that endangers others.

Event: M.M. leaves the team after the Herogasm/Soldier Boy debacle
Explanation: Walking away protects his psyche somewhat but weakens the group during a critical fight.

Event: M.M. works at a juvenile detention facility as a counselor
Explanation: Stable legal work gives him purpose while constructively helping troubled youths.

Event: M.M. hides his work with The Boys from his wife
Explanation: Secrecy protects her from legal fallout but erodes trust and strains the marriage.

Event: M.M. accepts Butcher’s offer to rejoin only when they have a real chance
Explanation: He trades personal safety for a realistic shot at accountability, benefiting himself and society.

Event: M.M. keeps the team alive and together as “The Heart”
Explanation: His stabilizing role increases survival and success despite emotional burden on him.

Event: As leader, Milk avoids risky plays and the team nearly gets killed for minimal gain
Explanation: His overcautious yet ineffective decisions endanger the team without achieving results.

Event: Milk fails to act as a “bastard” leader, prompting members to disobey
Explanation: Weak authority undermines cohesion and effectiveness, hurting both him and the team.

Event: M.M. leaves Frenchie in jail when Frenchie turns himself in
Explanation: He respects Frenchie’s choice and avoids interfering legally, neither clearly helping nor harming others.

Event: Butcher has Mallory pull strings to free Frenchie and force him back to work
Explanation: He regains a skilled operative while trampling Frenchie’s agency and exposing him to danger.

Event: M.M. nearly runs away to Belize with his family after burnout
Explanation: Choosing retreat would prioritize their safety and sanity over a losing war.

Event: A-Train gives M.M. a pep talk that keeps him from fleeing
Explanation: Encouraging him to stay in the fight restores his resolve and helps the broader anti-Vought cause.

Event: M.M. regularly breaks up fights at the juvenile facility
Explanation: He protects the kids from harm while maintaining order and doing his job well.

Event: M.M. forbids inmates from using the N-word
Explanation: Discouraging the slur promotes respect and a healthier environment with little downside.

Event: M.M. is disturbed by disorder and lets that push him to “fix” things
Explanation: His OCD-driven need to organize helps others sometimes but harms his own mental well-being.

Event: Frenchie uses M.M.’s dislike of disorder to recruit him to help Kimiko
Explanation: Manipulating his trait nudges him into aiding Kimiko, helping her and the team overall.

Event: M.M. and Annie go to Herogasm together to evacuate people
Explanation: He enters danger to get civilians out, risking himself for strong benefit to others.

Event: M.M. glares at Hughie when Hughie and Starlight wake him
Explanation: He asserts boundaries and authority, mildly intimidating Hughie without real gain for others.

Event: M.M. initially refuses to release the Female when he first meets her
Explanation: Detaining a dangerous unknown protects the team’s safety while restricting her freedom.

Event: M.M. works as a juvenile detention counselor instead of full-scale vigilantism
Explanation: Choosing smaller, legitimate impact improves his life and still does tangible good.

Event: M.M. scolds Hughie for meeting with Starlight while a fugitive
Explanation: His harsh warning lowers risk to Hughie, the team, and Starlight.

Event: M.M. calls out Billy’s abusive treatment of Hughie
Explanation: Confronting Butcher protects Hughie’s well-being and helps rein in destructive behavior.

Event: M.M. opposes Hughie’s relationship with Starlight on security grounds
Explanation: Discouraging the relationship reduces risk of Vought discovering them and endangering both.

Event: M.M. becomes increasingly aggressive toward Hughie in Season 2
Explanation: His hostility hurts their relationship and doesn’t improve outcomes for anyone.

Event: M.M. stops acting hostile after Hughie calls him out
Explanation: Adjusting his behavior restores team morale and trust.

Event: M.M. again bullies and dismisses Hughie as leader in Season 4
Explanation: He undermines his own leadership by alienating a key ally without gaining strategic value.

Event: M.M. intentionally tries to keep Hughie and Annie from getting back together
Explanation: Blocking their reunion reduces operational risk to the team and to Annie.

Event: M.M. intimidates juvenile inmates in his introduction
Explanation: Using intimidation keeps volatile teens in line and maintains safety in a hard environment.

Event: M.M. throws knockout gas at Soldier Boy
Explanation: He endangers himself taking a shot that fails and doesn’t slow Soldier Boy.

Event: M.M. repeatedly tries to take on Soldier Boy despite standing no chance
Explanation: His revenge fixation jeopardizes him and the team without meaningful effect on Soldier Boy.

Event: M.M. serves as Butcher’s right-hand man and contradicts him when needed
Explanation: Balancing support with pushback keeps operations effective and somewhat restrained.

Event: M.M. takes over leadership of The Boys when Butcher is demoted
Explanation: He steps up to fill a needed role, though his later performance is mixed.

Event: M.M. becomes stubborn and short-tempered when in charge
Explanation: His rigidity and aggression lower team performance and morale without offsetting gains.

Event: M.M. uses his trap-making skills to booby-trap Judy’s home against Black Noir
Explanation: The traps improve survival odds for him and the team while impeding a lethal enemy.

Event: M.M. flips A-Train to their side as leader
Explanation: Recruiting A-Train gives the team intel and protection, and allows A-Train partial redemption.

Event: M.M. feels sympathy over Todd’s death despite disliking him
Explanation: Empathy costs him little and indicates stable morality, slightly improving his relational stance with family.

Event: M.M. intentionally plays “Team Mom,” caring for the Boys
Explanation: His caretaking keeps everyone functioning and united at emotional cost to himself.
